goal: Generate frontend pages using retrieved design knowledge rather than generic template instincts.
steps: Read the task and identify page type, visual style, components, motion needs, and constraints.
Search UI knowledge for page type, style, component patterns, and motion patterns.
Create a short design brief from retrieved chunks: layout, components, states, motion, accessibility, avoid rules.
Implement the page in the existing tech stack without adding dependencies unless explicitly allowed.
Include meaningful states: loading, empty, error, success, selected, hover/focus where relevant.
Run available build/static checks and visually inspect if tooling exists.
prompt_contract: Retrieved chunks are constraints, not decoration suggestions.
If chunks conflict, prioritize direct_spec over screenshot_observed over summary_only.
If no relevant chunk exists, say what was missing and proceed with a conservative design.
verification: Design is not a generic flat template
Run/build command succeeds or failure is reported
failure_modes: Agent copies the example text but not the design rules

quality.notes: Tested conceptually with Claude-generated Liquid Glass page using local DB search.

retrieval.ai_summary: For frontend generation, retrieve page/style/component/motion chunks first, synthesize a design brief, implement with existing stack, include states and accessibility, then report how chunks shaped the page.
